source: All-In Podcast — E278: AI memory crunch, Micron blowout, NYC socialists, SpaceX float (w/ Kalanick)
summary: Anthropic is worth approximately $3 trillion as a public company today
summary_extended: Baker argues that Anthropic, if it were publicly traded today, would command a market capitalization of approximately $3 trillion, making it one of the most valuable companies in the world. His reasoning is grounded in projected 2025 revenues well above $100 billion, a strong growth trajectory toward $200-300 billion in revenue by 2028, and approximately 85% gross margins on inference at scale — a combination he views as justifying an extremely high valuation multiple. Baker frames this not as a supply/demand distortion from private market dynamics but as a genuine reflection of intrinsic value that public markets would also recognize. The investment implication is primarily observational given Anthropic's private status, but Baker's view signals that AI model providers operating at scale with high gross margins represent a new category of hyper-valued software asset. No directly investable US-listed equity is the subject of this thesis, as Anthropic remains private with no publicly traded pure-play equivalent.
detail: Baker estimates Anthropic would trade at ~$3 trillion if public, based on projected 2025 revenue well over $100B, strong 2028 growth trajectory (possibly $200-300B), and ~85% gross margins on inference at scale. He views the private-to-public transition as a simple market shift, not a supply/demand problem for capital markets.
confidence_evidence: Baker states '$3 trillion' directly and unprompted, provides revenue and margin reasoning
